events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need assess gas meter readings. Image: O2 20.9 %VOL, LEL 13%, H2S 1 ppm, CO 5 ppm. Need acceptable entry limits per OSHA 1910.146. Oxygen acceptable between 19.5% and 23.5%. LEL must be less than 10% for entry (per OSHA permit-required confined space, atmospheric conditions: safe for entry if O2 19.5-23.5, flammable gas vapor mist less than 10% LEL, toxic substances within permissible exposure limits PEL or other acceptable limit; >10% LEL not acceptable). H2S: OSHA PEL ceiling 10 ppm? Actually general industry PEL for H2S: ceiling 10 ppm (8hr TWA none? 10 ppm ceiling). 1 ppm acceptable. CO: OSHA PEL 50 ppm TWA, 35 ppm ceiling? Actually 1910.1000 Table Z-1: CO TWA 50 ppm, ceiling 200 ppm. 5 ppm acceptable. LEL 13% > 10% => not acceptable. Decision fail. Risk high because flammable atmosphere. Work_stage planned. percent_complete maybe 0 or based on pre-entry testing? It's planned scoped not started, pre-entry test is part of permit testing; percent_complete 0? Could be 10? Likely 0 since not started. component_conditions none flagged. value maybe LEL 13. Findings: LEL reading 13% exceeds 10% allowable entry limit; flammable atmosphere at mid-depth. Actions: ventilate/purge/re-test until LEL <10% and stable, verify O2, toxics; do not authorize entry. References: OSHA 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(B?) etc. Need produce JSON with all fields. Ensure valid. confidence high 1.0. event_time/rate/order/sound_source not used; but schema wants values. sound_source "" maybe. event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order [] and workable [] ok. The instructions: Do not include prose outside JSON. But final answer must be raw JSON. We should include only JSON. No markdown. The system says response must be raw JSON. So final: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "planned", ...}. We need include references. Also note H2S and CO within limits; O2 normal. Could also mention LEL 13% could be due to residual vapors. Risk high not critical? If LEL 13% near explosive, no immediate ignition source unknown; high. Decision fail. Let's craft. Ensure value numeric. The task asks identify any reading outside acceptable entry limits; value perhaps 13. Use value for numeric reading or computed quantity. Could be 13. confidence 1.0.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "planned",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 13,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "O2 reading 20.9% is within acceptable range (19.5%–23.5%).",
    "LEL reading is 13%, which exceeds the 10% LEL entry limit.",
    "H2S reading 1 ppm is below the OSHA ceiling of 10 ppm.",
    "CO reading 5 ppm is below the OSHA PEL/TWA of 50 ppm.",
    "Atmosphere at mid-depth is not safe for entry due to elevated flammable gas concentration."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not authorize confined space entry.",
    "Ventilate, purge, or otherwise control the flammable atmosphere.",
    "Re-test at multiple depths/locations until LEL is below 10% and readings are stable.",
    "Verify all readings remain within acceptable limits before signing the permit."
  ],
  "rationale": "The pre-entry 4-gas monitor shows O2 (20.9%), H2S (1 ppm), and CO (5 ppm) within acceptable limits, but the LEL reading of 13% exceeds the OSHA-permissible 10% threshold for confined space entry. A flammable atmosphere exists, so entry cannot be authorized until the concentration is reduced and re-testing confirms compliance.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) - acceptable entry conditions require atmospheric oxygen, flammable gas/vapor/mist below 10% LEL, and toxics within PEL/acceptable limits",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(d)(5)(iii) - testing conducted before entry and continuous monitoring"
  ]
}
